free_months = subscription_data.get('free_months')
assert free_months is None or free_months == 0, f"Subscription has free months: {free_months}"
framework_logger.info("Verified subscription has no free months")

violations.current_code: # Step 10 and 12: send_rtp_devicestatus called once per step
violations.expected_code:
# send_rtp_devicestatus should be called multiple times to simulate actual page printing
# For step 10: call 6 times to print 6 pages
# For step 12: call 9 times to print 9 additional pages
violations.explanation: Steps 10 and 12 call send_rtp_devicestatus() only once per step, but the test case requires printing specific numbers of pages (6 pages in step 10, 9 more pages in step 12). The RTP device status should be sent multiple times to accurately simulate the page count, or the method should be modified to accept a page count parameter.

fix_details.replace_with:
# Send RTP device status multiple times to simulate actual page printing
for _ in range(6):  # Print 6 pages
    common.send_rtp_devicestatus(
        entity_id=printer_data.entity_id,
        cloud_id=printer_data.cloud_id,
        device_uuid=printer_data.device_uuid
    )
    page.wait_for_timeout(1000)  # Small delay between prints

violations.current_code:
# Locators defined as module-level strings:
plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
# ... etc
violations.expected_code:
# Locators should be defined inside Elements inner class:
class Elements:
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    # ... etc
violations.explanation: CRITICAL STRUCTURAL VIOLATION: The page additions file defines locators as module-level strings instead of inside an Elements inner class. According to the framework pattern shown in the merge instructions and coding standards, locators must be defined as class attributes inside an Elements inner class that is nested within the page object class. The current structure will not work when merged into PrintHistoryPage because the page object expects self.elements.locator_name pattern. The file shows 'ADD TO PrintHistoryPage.Elements CLASS' in comments but then defines the locators at module level, which is incorrect.

fix_details.description: Restructure file to define locators inside Elements class
fix_details.required_structure:
class Elements:
    # String selector definitions as class attributes
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    complimentary_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-progress-bar']"
    complimentary_pages_value = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-value']"
    complimentary_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon']"
    complimentary_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-message']"
    complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
    additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
    additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"

violations.current_code:
# __init__ additions create Locator objects directly:
self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(self.elements.plan_pause_info)
self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator(self.elements.billing_cycle_period_card)
# ... etc
violations.expected_code:
# For Elements-based locators, do NOT create Locator properties in __init__
# Access via page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator_name) in test scripts
# OR if creating properties, they should be @property methods that return page.locator()
violations.explanation: CRITICAL PATTERN VIOLATION: The __init__ additions create Locator object properties (self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(...)) which contradicts the Elements class pattern. When locators are defined in the Elements inner class as strings, they should be accessed in test scripts via page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator_name), NOT as direct properties. Creating these properties defeats the purpose of the Elements container pattern and creates confusion about which access pattern to use. The framework uses two patterns: (1) Direct Locator properties defined in __init__ for simple cases, (2) Elements class with string selectors accessed via page.locator(). This file mixes both patterns incorrectly.

fix_details.lines_to_remove:
self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(self.elements.plan_pause_info)
self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator(self.elements.billing_cycle_period_card)
self.complimentary_pages_progress_bar = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_progress_bar)
self.complimentary_pages_value = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_value)
self.complimentary_pages_info_icon = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_info_icon)
self.complimentary_pages_info_message = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_info_message)
self.complimentary_pages_tooltip = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_tooltip)
self.additional_pages_progress_bar = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_progress_bar)
self.additional_pages_value = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_value)
self.additional_pages_info_icon = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_info_icon)
self.additional_pages_info_message = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_info_message)
self.additional_pages_tooltip = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_tooltip)
fix_details.explanation: Remove all __init__ property initializations. Locators defined in Elements class should be accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator_name) pattern in test scripts, not as direct properties.

The instructions also include __init__ additions that contradict the Elements pattern. This creates confusion for developers trying to merge the additions. The instructions should be clearer about the correct pattern and should not include contradictory code.

2. Locate the PrintHistoryPage class definition
3. Find the Elements inner class (defined inside PrintHistoryPage)
4. Add the string selector definitions below to the Elements class (after existing selectors)
5. Locate the __init__ method in PrintHistoryPage
6. After the line 'super().__init__(page)' and after 'self.elements = self.Elements()', add the Locator property initializations
7. Maintain consistent 4-space indentation throughout
8. Save the file and run the test to verify all locators work correctly
9. Update tooltip selectors if actual data-testid attributes differ from the placeholders

MERGE INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Open pages/print_history_page.py in your editor
2. Locate the PrintHistoryPage class definition
3. Find the Elements inner class (defined inside PrintHistoryPage)
4. Add the string selector definitions below to the Elements class as class attributes
5. Do NOT add any __init__ property initializations - Elements-based locators are accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator_name) in test scripts
6. Maintain consistent 4-space indentation throughout
7. Save the file
8. Update test scripts to access locators via page.locator(print_history_page.elements.locator_name) pattern
9. Update tooltip selectors if actual data-testid attributes differ from the placeholders
10. Run the test to verify all locators work correctly

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: CRITICAL FIX REQUIRED: All locators defined in the page_additions Elements class are being accessed incorrectly as direct properties (print_history_page.locator_name) instead of via the .elements. namespace (page.locator(print_history_page.elements.locator_name)). This is the framework's Pattern B for Elements containers. Every instance must be replaced with the correct access pattern. This requires 31 separate REPLACE_CODE operations, each replacing a direct property access with the page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator_name) pattern.

CRITICAL STRUCTURAL FIX: The page_additions file must be completely restructured. Locators are currently defined as module-level strings but must be defined inside an Elements inner class. Create a proper class Elements: block and move all string selector definitions inside it as class attributes. This is a RESTRUCTURE operation that changes the entire file structure.

CRITICAL PATTERN FIX: Remove all __init__ property initializations from the page_additions file. When locators are defined in the Elements class as strings, they should NOT have corresponding Locator properties created in __init__. The test scripts should access them via page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator_name). Remove all 12 lines of self.locator_name = page.locator(self.elements.locator_name) code.

CRITICAL METHOD FIX: The method GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription_via_rails_admin() does not exist in the codebase. Replace this call with the correct sequence of existing Rails Admin methods: access_edit_menu(), select appropriate pause event/state, save changes, and verify with verify_rails_admin_info(). This requires understanding the Rails Admin workflow for pausing subscriptions.

Add explicit verification that subscription has no free months immediately after getting subscription_data. Insert assertion code: free_months = subscription_data.get('free_months'); assert free_months is None or free_months == 0; framework_logger.info('Verified subscription has no free months'). This ensures precondition 2 is properly validated.

Step 20 visual verification is incomplete. Add TODO comment and implementation notes for integrating visual regression testing tool (Applitools, Percy, or similar). The current implementation only captures a screenshot but does not compare against baseline. Add code to load baseline image, perform comparison, and assert visual match.

Remove unused import: from pages.confirmation_page import ConfirmationPage. This page object is only used once for a simple visibility check that could be done with a direct locator or removed entirely. Removing the import reduces dependencies.

Steps 10 and 12 call send_rtp_devicestatus() only once but should call it multiple times to accurately simulate printing the specified number of pages. Wrap the call in a for loop: for _ in range(6): common.send_rtp_devicestatus(...); page.wait_for_timeout(1000). Do the same for step 12 with range(9).

Use existing common.validate_subscription_state() helper method instead of manually getting subscription data and validating state. This helper includes retry logic and proper polling. Replace manual validation with: common.validate_subscription_state(subscription_id, 'subscribed').

Update merge instructions in the page_additions file docstring to correctly describe the Elements class pattern. Remove contradictory instructions about __init__ property initializations. Clarify that locators should be added to Elements class as string attributes and accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator_name) in test scripts.

MINOR STYLE IMPROVEMENT: Consolidate verbose logging in precondition setup and steps 10/12. While the current logging is functional, reducing to 1-2 log lines per major action would improve readability. This is a low-priority style improvement that does not affect functionality.
